rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert new annex content only within a valid owner scope.
Use component type scope for contracts over component features; use implementation scope only for implementation-level bindings or assignments valid there.
Do not move, rename, or alter unrelated features, subcomponents, connections, or properties while merging generated content.
generic_rules: Keep ownership and scope consistent: references in a merged block must be valid for the enclosing component or implementation.
When fusing content, preserve all existing architecture text and make the smallest structural change needed to add the new block.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level assignments belong in implementation scope; guarantee-style contract statements do not.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only bindings in a component type scope or contract clauses in an implementation scope.
Do not introduce references to subcomponent ports or out-of-scope names in the merged block.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were automatically visible AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: validator type-fix tactics beyond generic scope preservation
case-specific threshold or clause selection decisions
